messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS\nOriginal request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text \"hello world\" colors, through a frontend ui\nClarifications:\n- framework: Express\n- styling: Tailwind CSS\nPrevious validation issues:\nNone",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "init-project",
      "title": "Initialize project repository",
      "description": "Create the project directory, run npm init -y, and initialize a git repository with a README placeholder.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "A new project folder exists containing package.json and a .git directory; README.md placeholder is present."
    },
    {
      "id": "install-dependencies",
      "title": "Install server and build dependencies",
      "description": "Install Express and development tooling: tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer, and optional dev tools like nodemon and concurrently.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "package.json lists express, tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer and devDependencies installed; node_modules populated and no install errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "configure-tailwind",
      "title": "Configure Tailwind CSS build",
      "description": "Initialize Tailwind and PostCSS configs, create a src/styles/input.css with @tailwind directives, and configure output directory for compiled CSS.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "tailwind.config.js and postcss.config.js exist; an input CSS file with @tailwind directives is present; running the build produces a compiled CSS file in the public/dist folder."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-express-server",
      "title": "Create Express server to serve static assets and API",
      "description": "Add server.js (or app.js) that serves the frontend static files and exposes a minimal JSON API (GET /api/color, POST /api/color) to read/update the current text color in memory.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Server starts without errors and serves static files; GET /api/color returns a JSON color value and POST /api/color updates it (confirmed with curl or browser)."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-frontend-ui",
      "title": "Build frontend UI with Tailwind to display and change color",
      "description": "Create index.html and a small app.js that render the 'hello world' text, provide a color picker and preset buttons, fetch initial color from the API, and POST updates when the user changes the color.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "configure-tailwind",
        "create-express-server"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "high",
      "successCriteria": "The page displays 'hello world' and the UI lets a user change its color; color changes update the displayed text immediately and persist via the API (verified across reloads)."
    },
    {
      "id": "wire-build-and-dev-scripts",
      "title": "Add npm scripts for build and development",
      "description": "Add npm scripts: build:css, watch:css, start, and dev (concurrently runs server and tailwind watch) so the project can be run and developed easily.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "configure-tailwind",
        "create-express-server"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "npm run build:css produces compiled CSS; npm run dev launches both the Express server and tailwind watcher without errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "test-and-documentation",
      "title": "Test app and write README",
      "description": "Manually test functionality (UI, API, build scripts) and add a README with setup, run, and usage instructions plus example curl commands for the API.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "create-frontend-ui",
        "wire-build-and-dev-scripts"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "All listed features work end-to-end on a fresh clone following the README; README contains commands to install, run, and test the color change behavior."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 7,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down functional decomposition: first prepare environment and dependencies, then configure Tailwind, implement the Express backend, build the frontend UI to change text color, add convenience scripts, and finish with testing and documentation."
}
